description: The Forty Thieves were a New York City criminal street gang formed in 1825 by Edward Coleman, primarily composed of Irish immigrants and Irish Americans. Based in the Five Points neighborhood of Manhattan, they engaged in street fighting, assault, murder, and robbery. The gang dissolved by 1850, with members joining larger gangs or leaving.

country_links.evidence_quote: Another criminal gang named the 'Forty Thieves' which had no criminal ties to the New York gang was formed in London , England in 1828.
country_links.context: A separate, unrelated gang named the Forty Thieves was formed in London, England.
